text: Initial survey of thoracic cavities conducted via endoscope.
On lifting upper lobe of left lung, multiple hydrated cysts were observed along the fissure.
Interlobar adhesions were released.
Citalon soak gauze pieces were used to cordon off the area.
Cysts were aspirated with an aspiration needle and are cut open exposing the germinal membranes contained within the lung parenchyma.
Germinal membrane was safely delivered over gauze piece and gently placed in an endo bag, avoiding spillage in thoracic cavity.
Individual membranes are plucked, delivered out and placed in sterile endobag.
This meticulous delivery helps to restrict widespread dissemination of hydrated disease and possible lethal anaphylactic reaction.
Pulsating pericardium is seen in close relation to hydrated cyst.
The adhesions are taken down with ultrasonic energy device.
Hydrated cyst is punctured accurately and delivered out in a secured manner.
Dissection at this juncture is a challenging task as any error in the judgment would exponentially increase the risk of catastrophe.
An intimate knowledge of the compact anatomy of mediastinum is essential to ensure a safe passage.
Delivery of the final cyst signaled the halfway point of our surgery.
Post-excision hypertonic saline washes were given to prevent recurrence of hydrated disease.
Sterile endobag is then delivered out.
Intercostal drain tube was inserted after complete hemostasis.
After an initial inspection, the greater curvature was dissected off the greater omen dump to approach the spleen.
The cyst located in the splenic lower pole was observed to be adherent to the splenic flexure of transverse column and lateral abdominal wall.
Citalon soak gauze pieces are introduced.
In view of dense adhesions and location of the cyst, preoperative spleen conserving surgery was planned.
Aspiration needle was introduced to confirm the cyst location.
Turbid fluid was aspirated.
The cyst was laid open and multiple daughter cysts were retrieved from the cyst cavity.
A similar meticulous delivery of the live daughter cysts is performed into the endobag.
The soft and gelatinous consistency of the germinal membrane necessitates atraumatic instrumentation and handling.
The germinal membrane is retrieved en masse from the cyst cavity and reposited into the bag.
Cluster of daughter cysts are observed at the lower pole of spleen.
These daughter cysts are serially aspirated one after another with the suction cannula.
It was imperative to carry out complete clearance as daughter cyst remnants can lead to recurrence of hydrated cysts.
After ensuring suction of all daughter cysts, a thorough visual inspection is performed to document completeness of the process.
The cyst cavity is then laid open and dissected from the lateral abdominal wall.
A lavage with citalon solution is given and irrigated with normal saline.
A thorough cleansing brings about completion of procedure.
Fomental plug was patched into the cavity to obliterate the dead space.
Peritoneal drain was placed.
